Code Block Top Glow Line (global.css lines 130-137) ```css .prose pre::before { content: ""; position: absolute; inset: 0 0 auto 0; height: 1px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, var(--color-accent-glow) 50%, transparent); opacity: 0.6; } ``` A **horizontal accent glow line** across the top of code blocks. #### D. Card Top Border Glow on Hover (global.css lines 232-249) ```css .card::before { content: ""; position: absolute; inset: 0 0 auto 0; height: 1px; background: var(--color-border-bright); opacity: 0.5; transition: opacity 0.2s ease, background 0.2s ease; } .card:hover::before { background: var(--color-accent); opacity: 0.6; } ``` Cards have a **subtle top border that transitions to accent blue on hover**. #### F. Blinking Cursor Animation (global.css lines 263-274) ```css .cursor::after { content: "▊"; display: inline-block; margin-left: 0.25rem; color: var(--color-accent); animation: blink 1.1s steps(2, start) infinite; transform: translateY(-2px); } @keyframes blink { to { opacity: 0; } } ``` A **terminal-style blinking cursor** effect (used via the `.cursor` class, though not currently applied in any template). #### G. Button Glow on Hover (global.css lines 293-311) ```css .btn-primary:hover { color: var(--color-accent); background: var(--color-surface-raised); box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px var(--color-accent); } ``` The primary button gets an **accent-colored inset border glow** on hover. #### I. HR Glow Divider (global.css lines 379-384) ```css .hr-glow { height: 1px; border: none; background: linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, var(--color-border-bright) 20%, var(--color-accent) 50%, var(--color-border-bright) 80%, transparent); opacity: 0.5; } ``` A **horizontal rule with a centered accent glow** that fades to transparent on both sides. Used in `BlogPost.astro`. #### J. Section Heading Gradient Line (global.css lines 217-222) ```css .section-heading::after { content: ""; flex: 1; height: 1px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--color-border-bright), transparent); } ``` Section headings have a **gradient line extending to the right**. #### K. Mobile Nav Glow Line (Header.astro line 46) ```html
``` The mobile dropdown nav has a **gradient accent glow line at the top**. #### L. Footer Gradient Divider (Footer.astro line 13) ```html
``` A **subtle gradient divider** at the top of the footer. #### M. Icon Button Hover Effects (global.css lines 386-401) ```css .icon-btn { transition: color 0.2s ease, background 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease, transform 0.15s ease; } .icon-btn:hover { color: var(--color-accent); background: var(--color-surface-raised); } ``` Icon buttons have a **transform transition** defined (though no transform is applied on hover currently -- it is ready for a scale/translate effect). #### N. Hero Image Shadow (BlogPost.astro line 58) ```html ``` Blog hero images use the **`--shadow-elevated`** pattern inline. --- ### 6. Overall Design Aesthetic and Color Scheme **Aesthetic: "Dark Terminal / Developer Portfolio"** The design is a **dark-mode, developer-centric aesthetic** inspired by terminal/IDE interfaces. Key characteristics: - **Dark, near-black backgrounds** with very subtle blue undertones - **Monospace typography** for labels, navigation, dates, tags, and buttons (JetBrains Mono) - **Sans-serif body text** (Inter with OpenType features `ss01`, `cv11`) - **Terminal-inspired UI patterns**: `$ whoami` labels, `//` section prefixes, `>` prompt markers, blinking cursor class - **Minimal, sharp-cornered design** (border-radius: 3-4px everywhere) - **Subtle depth through inset highlights** (`box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.03-0.04)`) simulating light from above - **Sans:** Inter (400, 500, 600, 700) -- body, headings - **Mono:** JetBrains Mono (400, 500) -- all UI chrome, labels, code **Transition Patterns** (consistent across the codebase): - Color transitions: `0.15s ease` - Background/shadow transitions: `0.15s-0.2s ease` - Transform transitions: `0.15s ease` (defined but sparingly used) **Summary:** This is a meticulously crafted dark developer portfolio with a cohesive "terminal meets modern UI" aesthetic. The accent blue (`#38bdf8`) is the sole color accent against a palette of 4 near-black surface shades and 4 gray text shades. Glow effects are used sparingly but intentionally -- primarily as 1px highlight lines, subtle box-shadows on hover, and a background ambient radial gradient. The design prioritizes subtlety and restraint over flashy effects.

parts.text:
Build passes. Here's what I added — all subtle, all using the existing `--color-accent-glow` token:

| Element | Effect |
|---|---|
| **Portrait ring** | Accent border + soft outer glow on hover |
| **Icon buttons** | `box-shadow` glow on hover |
| **Rail dot** | Permanent soft glow halo (timeline dots) |
| **Links** | Faint `text-shadow` glow on hover |
| **Chip** | Left accent bar brightens + glows on hover; subtle outer glow on card |
| **btn-primary** | Outer glow added alongside the existing inset accent border |
| **Tags** | Tiny glow on hover |

All effects are restrained (negative spread values, low-opacity glow token) and only activate on hover (except the rail dot which is always-on but very subtle). Run `npm run dev` to preview.
